Uuh, wait, so, you have completed the medley as a whole already, or just something like a song order? Can't really tell based on how you wrote it  ^_^;
But anyway, I don't mind this at all, having people improve and play around with the FLP files is why I've released them in the first place. Just remember to link me the result when it's ready, I'd love to hear it.  :D

text: Well, you could try linking it on this message chain. Then we can take the link message down after I've downloaded it, if you want.
author: @FinnOtaku

text:
YUE 【ミュージックビデオ】 ...Wait, these song choices! So you were the one who made the medley for this collab, nice. :D https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f9RRvg_1wHQ

Well, both this and that collab's medley were both a bit rough from the edges, but I can see that you had improved the patterns quite nicely in this new medley compared to their usage in that collab. The song ordering could've been better though, some of the songs didn't flow that smoothly to one another. The mastering here isn't that good, but that one's mostly my fault.
All in all though, I'd say you're definitely on a right track for a full-fledged medley of your own. As patterns, the songs barely had any off-keys, and some of the arrangements were really nice despite the base I had provided. I especially liked how concon and Stickerbrush Symphony sounded like.  :)
It's pretty much about practice at this point. If you keep messing around in FL Studio like I did, you're bound to get better eventually.  :3
author: @FinnOtaku

text: +FinnOtaku thanks. and well, I didn't really make the medley for this collab. I just got inspired on this collab medley here so
author: @thehamarejun

text:
Wait, really? Sorry about that then, it just seemed so obvious with your name on that video's description and all.  XD
But yeah, I think you're still on the right track to get better with the FL STudio. Good luck with it.  :)
